game: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim Special Edition

keys.effect: Exclusive fullscreen mode: 0=windowed or borderless, 1=exclusive fullscreen
keys.notes: Set bFull Screen=0 together with bBorderless=1 for borderless windowed. Exclusive fullscreen may provide a minor GPU scheduling advantage on some hardware.

keys.effect: Borderless windowed mode: 0=off, 1=on. Must be paired with bFull Screen=0.
Shadow map texture resolution per cascade: 512, 1024, 2048, or 4096. Higher values produce sharper contact and object shadows at the cost of GPU memory and fill rate.
keys.notes: Default is 2048. 4096 is the practical maximum — the engine ignores values above this.

keys.effect: Volumetric lighting (god rays) from sunlight, interior torches, and particle light sources: 0=off, 1=on
Screen-space ambient occlusion — darkens crevices, contact areas, and recessed geometry to add perceived depth: 0=off, 1=on
Screen-space reflections on water surfaces and wet terrain: 0=off, 1=on

keys.notes: Recommended off in all goals. SSE motion blur is a simple radial effect that adds visual noise without meaningful immersion benefit.

keys.effect: Anisotropic texture filtering strength: 1=off, 2=2×, 4=4×, 8=8×, 16=16×. Sharpens textures viewed at oblique angles (stone floors, cliff faces, long corridors).
keys.notes: 16× AF has near-zero GPU cost on any modern discrete GPU. Reducing below 8 is rarely worth the quality trade-off.

keys.effect: Depth-of-field blur applied to objects outside the focal distance: 0=off, 1=on
keys.notes: Subjective. Many players disable it for visual clarity during combat. Enable for a cinematic presentation. ENB presets typically replace this with a higher-quality custom DoF.

keys.effect: Distance in game units at which grass begins to fade. Higher values extend the visible grass field toward the horizon.
keys.notes: Default is 7000. Values above 14000 have diminishing visual returns and a disproportionate CPU cost. Requires strong single-threaded CPU performance to push beyond 14000 without stuttering.

keys.effect: Minimum spacing in game units between individual grass objects. Lower values place objects closer together, producing denser, fuller ground coverage.
keys.notes: Default is 20. Values below 10 give imperceptible density gains while continuing to increase CPU cost. Must be a whole number.

engine_overrides.behaviour: The in-game Settings menu writes back to SkyrimPrefs.ini whenever the player exits the menu, overwriting all manual INI edits. The Bethesda launcher can also reset SkyrimPrefs.ini if it detects hardware changes.
engine_overrides.action: After applying INI tweaks, set SkyrimPrefs.ini as read-only (right-click the file → Properties → Read-only). This locks your values but prevents the game from saving any new in-game graphics changes. Alternatively, use BethINI — a community INI manager that re-applies your settings on each launch.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Set to your monitor's native resolution
manual_only_settings.reason: iSize W and iSize H in SkyrimPrefs.ini are overwritten by the in-game display menu. Set resolution via the game launcher or in-game menu rather than editing the INI directly.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Enable TAA for quality; use the Skyrim Upscaler mod (PureDark) to replace TAA with DLSS 2/3, FSR 2, or XeSS for the best quality-to-performance ratio
manual_only_settings.reason: Skyrim SE ships with TAA only. DLSS, FSR, and XeSS require the third-party Skyrim Upscaler mod. Native SMAA injection is available via ENB presets.

notes: Skyrim Special Edition runs on the 64-bit Creation Engine. Many INI settings carry over from Skyrim LE (32-bit), but some defaults differ.
DLSS, FSR, and XeSS are not natively supported. The Skyrim Upscaler mod by PureDark adds DLSS 2/3, FSR 2, and XeSS as TAA replacements.

ENB Series (ENBoost) is a widely-used post-processing injector that adds SSAO, DoF, colour grading, and other effects beyond the game's native pipeline. ENB has its own enbseries.ini configuration separate from the game INI files.

Community Shaders is a modern, open-source alternative to ENB that replaces Skyrim SE's rendering pipeline using compute shaders. It provides screen-space global illumination, screen-space shadows, terrain blending, and more with a lower performance overhead than ENB. It is incompatible with ENB — only one shader injector can be active at a time. Community Shaders is available on Nexus Mods.

SKSE64 (Skyrim Script Extender) is a near-universal prerequisite for advanced graphics mods, including Community Shaders, ENB-dependent presets, and many lighting overhauls. It does not modify any INI files but must be launched via skse64_loader.exe instead of the default game executable. Available at https://skse.silverlock.org/
BethINI is a community tool that provides a guided INI editor for Bethesda titles. It applies tested presets and prevents common misconfiguration errors — recommended for first-time INI editing.
The Anniversary Edition content added in November 2021 does not change the INI file structure or config paths.

Nexus Mods (https://www.nexusmods.com/skyrimspecialedition) is the primary repository for Skyrim SE mods, including thousands of graphics improvements covering textures, meshes, lighting, weather, and post-processing. Individual mod recommendations are outside ReFrame's scope — Nexus search and community collections are the best starting point for further graphical enhancement.
